H2.3-E.gemini_depth: Structural correspondence (reaction-transport coupling) / Surface analogy (switching and hysteresis mechanisms)

key_findings.autocatalytic_claim: SIGN_ERROR — HPMCAS releases H+ on dissolution (negative feedback, not positive). Autocatalysis only holds with a basic drug payload.
key_findings.switching_mode: CONFIRMED — sigmoid dissolution transition near pH 5.5 is the strongest claim; moving-boundary reaction-diffusion formalism is sound.
key_findings.buffer_mode: OVERSTATED — buffer capacity equation is formally identical across domains but calling HPMCAS a classical buffer overstates the effect.
key_findings.geochemical_bridge: INSPIRATIONAL_ONLY — structural analogy at reaction-transport level is real; volcanic glass dual-buffer mechanism is not the mechanistic source.
key_findings.hysteresis_prediction: TESTABLE_BUT_UNCERTAIN — possible with structural memory; mechanisms in geo vs pharma are mathematically distinct.
key_findings.novel_gemini_prediction: Bulk buffer diffusivity should modulate apparent pH_switch: replacing high-diffusivity (phosphate) with low-diffusivity (maleate/polymeric) buffer at identical bulk pH and beta should shift the switch point.

agreement_areas: Switching behavior (Region 2) is the defensible core
Geochemical bridge is inspirational, not derivational
Autocatalytic claim has a sign error for HPMCAS alone
Hysteresis is testable but not guaranteed by acid-base chemistry alone
Buffer capacity equation (beta) is formally identical across domains
divergence_areas: Confidence scores: GPT 4/10 (mechanism claims overstated) vs Gemini 7/10 (structural framework is sound) — different evaluation axes
Gemini derived a novel diffusivity-ratio prediction not in the original hypothesis
